name: Cajun Skillet Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon fresh thyme or ½ teaspoon dried thyme
1 tablespoon chopped fresh oregano or ½ teaspoon dried oregano
1 tablespoon chopped fresh basil or ½ teaspoon dried basil
4 cups cooked black-eyed peas (about 32 oz. canned; ensure well drained)
recipeInstructions:
To simplify the process, I typically use dried, canned spices and a can of peeled tomatoes.
1. Start by sautéing onions and garlic until they are soft.
2. Add celery and bell peppers and continue to sauté until these vegetables are also soft.
3. Incorporate thyme, black pepper, cayenne pepper, oregano, basil, honey or molasses, and Dijon mustard. Stir to combine and let the spices cook for a few minutes until the onions become golden in color.
4. Add the chopped tomatoes (reserve any juices) and simmer the mixture, covered, for about 5 minutes.
5. Finally, stir in the cooked black-eyed peas and allow everything to heat through, which should take an additional 2-3 minutes.
